narration: Let's see constraints in action. Imagine a users table with a PRIMARY KEY on user_id. Every user gets a unique identifier. Add a NOT NULL constraint on email—no user can exist without contact information. A UNIQUE constraint on username prevents duplicates. Now add an orders table with a FOREIGN KEY linking to users. You cannot create an order for a non-existent user. Add a CHECK constraint ensuring order_total is greater than zero. These constraints work silently in the background, preventing bad data from entering your system.
